text: Recently Eastern Brahman Breeders Association (EBBA) - President, Cyana
Briles took a trip to Houston, Texas where the American Brahman Breeders
Association (ABBA) is Headquartered. Ms. Briles met with Ashley Hughes, the
Executive Vice President of the ABBA to thank our "parent organization" for all
their support in our endeavors out here on the East Coast in ABBA area 12.
Ashley Hughes agreed to present a program at our Eastern Brahman
Extravaganza Show in September at the NC Mountain State Fair discussing
While in the Texas area Ms. Briles also stopped and visited with Alfredo and
Josephina Muskus of the Santa Elena Ranch (Est.1959) located in Madisonville,
Texas. The Santa Elena Ranch has been raising quality Red Brahman Cattle for
many years. Alfredo Muskus is an esteemed ABBA judge and has graciously
agreed to come judge our Eastern Brahman Extravaganza show at his expense.
It is an honor to have a judge of his caliber offer to support our new show in such
a fashion. Alfredo Muskus has traveled the world from various countries in South
American to Thailand judging shows for the American Brahman Cattle Breed.
Alfredo Muskus has also agreed to organize a short presentation about exporting
Brahman Cattle to other countries, he has extensive expertise in this department.
This will be the Muskus' first visit to North Carolina and we are over the moon
excited to host them with our brand of southern style hospitality.
Infinity Cattle Services - Chris Shivers and Team
Next stop on this EBBA Texas tour was a meeting with Chris Shivers of Infinity
Cattle Services. This meeting was part networking and part interviewing Infinity
Cattle Services in regards to hosting our first Eastern Brahman Sale for us in the
Spring of 2020. Chris Shivers is well connected in the American Brahman World
having worked for the ABBA for 17 years, 13 of those years serving as the ABBA
Executive Vice President. The meeting was a delight and Chris Shivers vowed to
do all he could to help support our new organization, regardless of whether we
used his services to host a Brahman Sale or not. After a discussion among our
EBBA board members and a vote it was determined that with a goal of hosting a
high end quality Brahman Sale that we should indeed engage Infinity Cattle
Services to host our first EBBA Brahman Sale. The details are still being worked
out but please mark your calendar for a Saturday in Mid - May 2020 with the
tentative location somewhere in the state of Georgia. This will be a fantastic
opportunity for our EBBA membership to market their finest American Brahman
From the moment Ms. Briles first messaged this knowledgeable well respected
lady she knew she had found a "Mentor". Dinah Weil is a seasoned and savvy
cattle-woman who excels not only in her love of Red Brahman Cattle but in the art
of lifting people up by noticing and commenting on their positive characteristics.
The HK Cattle brand and all it has achieved over the years is in big part because
of Ms. Weil and the smart herds-woman and business-woman she is. This visit to
HK Cattle in Rosharon, TX solidified the friendship between Ms. Briles and Ms.
Weil but more importantly it garnered support for the passion that Ms. Briles has
in growing the Eastern Brahman Breeders Association.
We will be hosting an open Brahman Show at the North Carolina Mountain State
Fair in Fletcher, North Carolina at the WNC Agriculture Center on September 9,
2019. We are still in the planning stages working with the NC Department of
Agriculture and the Mountain State Fair officials and the ABBA.
We have our location, our date and our judge! A good start, but there is still so
much to do. To be a sanctioned ABBA points show we must first prove
ourselves, which means that we need a minimum of 40 registered head of
Brahman to show two years in a row. Our open show will feature both registered
Brahmans and a commercial class for all Brahman Influenced heifers. Our goal
for our show is that it be a friendly and fun show where everyone will feel
welcomed no matter their level of experience or pedigree of their cattle. We want
our Eastern Brahman Extravaganza to be a place where Brahman folks come to
network with other Brahman folks and a place where the general public can come
and learn more about our type of cattle. We have a show page on our website
Extravaganza where you can get all the information you need about our show,
classes, rules, a link to enter online and much more. Please check out all the
information on our show page and feel free to contact us if you have any
questions. Hope to see everyone there!
It is because of our amazing membership that we can host an open Brahman
Show. The Eastern Brahman Extravaganza is financed completely through
sponsorship funds. We will be mailing a sponsorship letter out to our membership
and we hope that you will consider a sponsorship. There will be options to
sponsor a Championship Buckle, hanging a banner in the arena (we will create
your banner for you and it's yours to keep), donating an item or service for the
silent auction along with many other options. Be on the lookout for your
sponsorship letter, but it can also be found on our website as a downloadable
PDF at the bottom of our show page Extravaganza.
Please help us grow our membership, if you know someone with Brahman or
Brahman Influenced Cattle, or if you sell a Brahman or Brahman Influenced
animal please tell folks about our Association. Your membership to the Eastern
Brahman Breeders Association helps help to market and promote us you
Brahman and Brahman Influenced Cattle in the Eastern part of the country. One
of the benefits of being a member is your listing on our Eastern Brahman
Website. Please check your listing for typos and that your links all work and that
we are featuring the image you want. It's easy to make updates and changes, just
send us an email or contact us through the website. In addition our website
features a "For Sale" page and every member is entitled to one free listing on the
For Sale page. There is a very simple form you can fill out on the For Sale page to
We have a Facebook page! Eastern Brahman Follow us and keep up to date on
all the latest Eastern Brahman Breeders news.
